title: mentha aquatica subvar. hirsuta
intro: Mentha aquatica subvar. hirsuta, also known as hairy water mint, is a subspecies of the water mint plant, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as indigestion, nausea, and diarrhea, due to its carminative and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica subvar. hirsuta include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate various health conditions.
